channel.title: Colorado on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Colorado on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Mutilation of "Snippy" the horse
item.description:
The 1967 death of an Appaloosa mare named “Snippy” near Alamosa, Colorado, gained national attention after reports suggested the animal had been mysteriously mutilated by extraterrestrial entities. While sensationalized media accounts linked the incident to UFOs, later investigations by the Condon Committee and others attributed the death to natural causes and gunshot wounds inflicted by local individuals. Despite these findings, the case remains a foundational element of UFO folklore and the study of alleged animal mutilations. The horse’s skeletal remains were ultimately acquired by the UFO Watchtower in 2021 as a tourist attraction.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutilation_of_%22Snippy%22_the_horse

item.title: UFO Watchtower
item.description:
The UFO Watchtower, located north of Hooper, Colorado, is an observation platform and campground that opened in May 2000. It was established by cattle farmer Judy Messoline, who converted her barn into a tourist attraction to capitalize on reported UFO sightings in the San Luis Valley. The attraction relies on donations and sales of alien-themed merchandise for funding. Over 30,000 visitors have reportedly visited the Watchtower since its opening, with reported sightings and a “healing garden” also being features.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_Watchtower

item.title: Dancing Blue Lights at Silver Cliff Cemetery
item.description:
Silver Cliff Cemetery, established in the 1880s near Silver Cliff, Colorado, is divided into Catholic and Protestant sections. The cemetery is known for reports of “dancing blue lights” that have been observed since the 1880s. These lights, described as blue lantern lights or white spheres, were featured in National Geographic Magazine in August 1969. Researcher Karen Stollznow suggested natural explanations for the lights, such as visual phenomena related to the human eye.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver_Cliff_Cemetery
